commit.message:
[gpos] Guard cursive attachment reversal

Cursive attachment reversal clears the old child edge before
walking the existing chain and reinstalling reversed links.
If the chain distance does not stay in range for
attach_chain(), reinstalling the reversed edge can truncate
and leave a poisoned chain value.

Pass the buffer length into the reversal helper so it drops
out-of-bounds chains, and use a round-trip fit check before
storing the reversed distance. When the reversed distance does
not fit, leave the old edge detached instead of corrupting
the chain.

Testing: CCACHE_DISABLE=1 meson test -C build --print-errorlogs
Fixes https://issues.chromium.org/issues/496302307
Assisted-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
refactor: fold comparator spec into BridgelandStability

Move the comparator challenge theorem from the separate BridgelandSpec library into BridgelandStability.Spec, remove the extra lean_lib entry, and update comparator/extractDeps/checkdecls/exposition wiring to use the in-library module. This lets CI answer whether comparator actually needs a separate library boundary.

- lake build BridgelandStability
- lake exe runLinter

commit.message:
🎨 Palette: [UX improvement] Add aria-labels to icon-only buttons in header (#914)

* 🎨 Palette: [UX improvement] Add aria-labels to icon-only buttons in header

I have added descriptive `aria-label` attributes to the icon-only `<button>` elements in the `ogabassey-header` component. This includes buttons for:
- Toggle menu (LayoutGrid)
- Clear search (X)
- Execute search (Search)
- Notifications (Bell)
- Shopping cart (ShoppingCart, dynamically including cart count)
- User account (User)

This ensures better screen reader accessibility and overall UX compliance.

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(a11y): handle singular/plural in cart aria-label

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Indexing Status Dialog with detailed error diagnostics (#96)

* docs: add indexing status dialog implementation plan

* test: scaffold indexing status dialog helpers

* feat: add indexing status dialog shell

* fix: tighten indexing status dialog source row rendering

* feat: lazily create indexing status dialog

* feat: add indexing status entry points

* feat: collect detailed indexing errors

* fix: align indexing error details with source status

* feat: forward indexing error details to dialog

* test: align indexing error details app test name

* feat: show recent indexing errors

* fix: truncate long error messages and add overflow accessibility

* fix: increase indexing status dialog height to 640px

* fix: truncate indexing error messages on utf-8 boundaries

* fix: reduce source count pill badge vertical size

* fix: rename "Skipped" to "Already Indexed" in indexing status dialog

"Skipped" had negative connotations (rejected/ignored). "Already Indexed"
is neutral and accurate: these sessions are in the index and were not
re-scanned because their fingerprint was unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove stale TODO and #[allow(dead_code)] on errors_detail field

The field is actively used by the indexing status dialog (Phase 2),
so the suppression and TODO(task-6) comment are no longer needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Revert "docs: add indexing status dialog implementation plan"

This reverts commit 6bd3a98a816fa8045504afed8ecea27d4ce06c07.

* fix: animate progress bar pulse during reindexing

pulse() was called only once in sync_widgets, so the bar never visibly
moved. Now a 250ms timer drives the animation while indexing is active
and is cleaned up when indexing completes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add doc comments to structs and enums in indexing_status.rs

Document non-obvious fields and types: lazy dialog creation, view-state
pattern, pulse timer, badge/subtitle rules, and error section constraints.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: reverse indexing error list to show most recent errors first

cf https://github.com/supermaciz/sessions-chronicle/pull/96#discussion_r2991986075

* fix: keep newest indexing errors with rolling cap and show most recent first

Replace early-return in push_indexing_error with remove(0) so the error
buffer discards the oldest entry instead of silently dropping new ones.
Reverse the iterator in build_recent_error_rows to display newest errors
first in the Recent Errors section.

Addresses PR #96 review comments r2991986070 and r2991986075.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: clarify indexing status recent errors

Rewrite generic Recent Errors messages to include the AI assistant and add coverage for the new formatting.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: skip empty claude sessions

Treat Claude Code sessions with no messages as ignorable during indexing while still surfacing genuinely malformed session files as errors.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: format generated config template

* fix(indexer): use VecDeque for O(1) error buffer operations

Replace Vec with VecDeque for the error_detail buffer to avoid O(n)
remove(0) operations when capping errors at MAX_INDEXING_ERRORS.
Now uses pop_front() for efficient FIFO eviction.

fix(ui): correct pluralization in overflow message

Show 'and 1 more error' instead of 'and 1 more errors' when exactly
one error remains beyond the visible limit.

* chore(ide): add Meson build directory profile in misc.xml

* fix: address PR #96 review findings

Move test-only _incremental indexer wrappers to #[cfg(test)] impl block,
remove spurious #[allow(dead_code)] on errors_detail field, document
qualify_error_message coupling, and treat NoUserMessages as empty session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: replace unavailable emblem-ok-symbolic with object-select-symbolic

emblem-ok-symbolic does not exist in the Adwaita icon theme; it only
appears in HighContrast without a -symbolic variant. Use the correct
Adwaita checkmark icon instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): consume published lean-exposition binary

Stop checking out and rebuilding lean-exposition inside the Exposition workflow. Instead, pin a published lean-exposition commit, download the matching Linux x86_64 artifact from its Publish Exposition Binary workflow, verify the packaged lean-toolchain, and run the extracted exposition binary directly under lake env.

Validation: lake exe cache get and lake build BridgelandStability succeeded in a clean worktree. lake exe runLinter was blocked locally by an ld.lld bus error while linking runLinter:exe on this aarch64 machine. The upstream lean-exposition publish workflow for e8fa65d41c5e8d3dc14f183dd30b97f784116bf2 succeeded, and the artifact download/extract plus lean-toolchain verification path was exercised locally. Executing the published binary locally is not possible on this machine because the artifact is Linux x86_64 and the host is aarch64.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Simplify training bootstrap and docs (#5)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): remove invalid exposition cache step

Drop the lake exe cache get invocation from the lean-exposition checkout in the Exposition workflow. That repo does not provide a cache executable, so the job was failing before it ever built the exposition tool.

Validated with lake exe cache get, lake build BridgelandStability, lake exe runLinter, lake build exposition in /home/matt.linux/lean/lean-exposition, and a fresh exposition generation run against BridgelandStability.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs: clarify required AI provenance disclosure

Spell out that all AI-assisted commits must disclose AI use, require exact co-author trailers for Codex and Claude when they materially author or edit patches, and require every applicable trailer when multiple AI systems contribute. Also record non-authoring AI help truthfully in the commit body or footer.

Validation: lake exe cache get succeeded. lake build BridgelandStability is currently blocked by an existing parse error at BridgelandStability/Deformation/DeformedGtLe.lean:444 (unexpected token 'GL'), so lake exe runLinter was not run.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Remove blueprint integration and add exposition CI

Drop the LeanArchitect dependency, remove the BridgelandBlueprint wrapper library and blueprint workflow, trim the project homepage links, and update extractDeps plus the new exposition workflow to use the slimmer library surface. Validated with lake exe cache get, lake build BridgelandStability, lake exe runLinter, and a fresh exposition run against the cleaned target.

AI-assisted via Codex.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

[claude] feat: enforce preflight validation before pipeline (#194)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(ui): redesign chess game interface

Summary:
- rebuild the main game view around a board-first layout with a
  compact top bar, integrated player strips, and a quieter side rail
- replace the shared visual system with a lighter refined-modern
  palette, updated typography, and cleaner responsive behavior
- refresh the setup, waiting, clock, and move history surfaces so the
  full play flow reads as one cohesive UI

Rationale:
- the previous UI gave the board, side rail, and status chrome nearly
  equal visual weight, so the game itself never felt primary
- regrouping controls and reducing surface noise makes casual play
  easier to scan while preserving existing functionality

Tests:
- npm test
- npm run lint
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Prefer real Vitest failure headers in fitness summaries and silence noisy hook-runtime test logs.

feat(host): expand session detail debug transcript tooling

Summary:
- capture full Codex debug transcript events and expose them on the
  session detail page behind Dashboard:DebugMode
- add per-method transcript filtering with optional delta tracking and
  move the filter panel outside the activity card
- compress session metadata into a sticky collapsible header strip and
  update docs and tests for the new layout and debug behavior

Rationale:
- operators need complete sent/received payload visibility to verify
  whether agents are following the workflow correctly
- method-level filters make high-volume transcript streams usable
  without hiding lifecycle and outcome events
- a compact sticky metadata strip frees horizontal space for the
  timeline while keeping the key counters visible during scrolling

Tests:
- dotnet test .\\dotnet\\tests\\Symphony.Host.IntegrationTests\\Symphony.Host.IntegrationTests.csproj -c Debug -m:1 -v minimal --no-restore --filter "FullyQualifiedName~SessionDetail" -p:BaseOutputPath=.\\dotnet\\artifacts\\adhoc2\\bin\\ -p:BaseIntermediateOutputPath=.\\dotnet\\artifacts\\adhoc2\\obj\\
- dotnet format .\\dotnet\\Symphony.sln --verify-no-changes --no-restore

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore: remove duplicate root asn_type.map and gitignore it (issue #67) (#68)

asn_type.map existed in two places: ./asn_type.map (root) and
data/asn_type.map. Both were identical (85 231 lines). The root copy
is a generated artifact from running bin/generate_asn_type_map.py
without --output (DEFAULT_OUTPUT defaults to asn_type.map in CWD).

No workflow, Dockerfile, or test reads ./asn_type.map directly —
the Dockerfile regenerates it at build time from the upstream URL.

Remove the root copy and gitignore the pattern so a local generator
run cannot accidentally re-commit it. data/asn_type.map stays as the
canonical reference copy.

Closes #67.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix stale event identity after board resubscribe

When the board reconnects after auth/session churn, local unconfirmed events could remain stamped with the previous session and author identity. The client would then keep retrying that stale pending batch after BoardSubscriptionCompleted, which matches the server-side failures where authorUserId no longer matches the authenticated user and the resend sequence drifts out of sync.

Refresh the identity on every unconfirmed local event as soon as the subscription handshake completes, and drop any stale pending send state before publish timers resume. This preserves local edits while ensuring the next outbound batch is rebuilt with the current connection sessionId and authorUserId.

Add a regression test covering the resubscribe path and keep the existing router/log tests green.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Keep interoperability roadmap lanes explicit until support is proven (#45)

The current mainline docs still left Aerial, cuMAC, and broader profile
expansion easy to read as general future support rather than bounded,
reviewable roadmap lanes. This patch updates the README and architecture
docs to separate hardened-now contract scaffolding from roadmap-only
interoperability claims, and adds a focused regression test so the
wording does not drift.

Constraint: Repo-visible docs must distinguish hardened-now support from roadmap-only claims
Rejected: Rely on issue tracker links alone | repo docs would still overstate support boundaries
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Do not promote Aerial, cuMAC, or broader-profile claims without updating these docs and the replacement contract docs test together
Tested: rg claim inventory across touched docs and the regression test; mix test apps/ran_action_gateway/test/ran_action_gateway/replacement_contract_examples_test.exs; mix contract_ci; git diff --check
Not-tested: GitHub PR checks on the fresh branch
Related: YON-56
Related: YON-58
Related: YON-59
Related: YON-60

security(proxy): add Origin-based CSRF protection in middleware (#899)

Replace per-route checkCsrfProtection() token pattern with a single
Origin header check in the middleware layer. This is the 2026 best
practice: modern browsers always send Origin on cross-origin mutation
requests, and SameSite=Lax cookies (Supabase default) provide
defense-in-depth.

Benefits:
- Covers ALL API routes automatically (vs ~100 individual calls)
- Zero client-side changes (no buildCsrfHeaders() wiring needed)
- No risk of breaking callers that don't send x-csrf-token
- Existing per-route checks remain as redundant defense-in-depth

Skip list: Bearer auth (mobile), webhooks, auth callbacks, cron jobs,
public analytics. Custom merchant domains are verified via domain cache.

Closes #898 (replaced approach — that PR would have broken logout and
loyalty redemption by requiring CSRF tokens callers don't send).

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: expand ActiveRecord schema extraction controls

User requests

- schemas add except option
- check whether ActiveRecord validation presence and allow_blank are extracted
- our gem allows changing the semantics of required, so keep that in mind when mapping presence
- check how validations with if and unless are handled
- schemas add an on option so validate on can be extracted
- schemas add a required option that can be true, false, or an array
- when required is an array, only those fields are required and every other field is treated as non-required
- document that rule in the README
- commit with co-author

Implementation

- add schemas(except: ...) and keep it composable with only:
- extract presence into allow_blank: false and avoid overloading it into required semantics alone
- skip validators with if:/unless: during static schema extraction
- support schemas(on: ...) so on:/except_on: validators can be extracted for a requested validation context
- add schemas(required: true | false | [fields]) as an explicit requiredness override for exported fields
- update README and README_zh with the new schemas options and semantics
- expand ActiveRecord schema specs to cover filtering, contexts, required overrides, and validator extraction edge cases

Verification

- bundle exec rspec

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>
